title: Dynamic Form Renderer
description: Build a renderer page/route that fetches a published form schema from the API and renders a single-page GOV.UK-styled form. Client-side required validation and a confirmation page on submit.
acceptanceCriteria: Renderer route/page accessible via URL with formId parameter (e.g., /render/:formId)
On load, fetches published schema from GET /api/forms/:formId/schema

Renders each field using the correct govuk-frontend component based on field type: text→text-input, textarea→textarea, date→date-input, number→text-input (inputmode=numeric), radio→radios, checkbox→checkboxes, select→select
Form title from schema displayed as <h1> using GOV.UK heading pattern
All rendered fields have visible labels with correct htmlFor/id association
Fields marked as required show '(required)' hint and are validated on submit
On submit with missing required fields: GOV.UK error summary shown at top of page with links to each error field
Error summary links focus to the relevant field on activation (WCAG 2.4.3)
On successful submit: structured JSON payload logged/posted to a confirmation endpoint
Confirmation page shows GOV.UK panel component with reference number and 'Form submitted' message
Full keyboard journey from start to submission works without mouse
GOV.UK page structure: header, phase banner (Beta), main content, footer
If schema fetch fails (404 or network error), show GOV.UK error page with clear message
definitionOfDone: Upload a PDF, edit fields, publish, then open renderer route — see a working GOV.UK form
Fill in the form, submit, see confirmation panel
Skip a required field, submit, see GOV.UK error summary
Navigate the entire form using only keyboard
Code passes TypeScript strict mode with no errors
Evaluator must run and produce a passing evaluation-04.json before sprint is marked complete
